qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Permissive CORS policy with default-off authentication enables cross-origin data injection and exfiltration via the engram HTTP server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same engram HTTP server root cause as the advisory: wildcard CORS, default-off authentication, and cross-origin /learn plus graph read routes on the local server.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded request body reading in HTTP server enables memory exhaustion denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same broad HTTP server area, but a different root cause and impact: unbounded body buffering DoS rather than wildcard CORS/default-off auth enabling CSRF graph exfiltration and persistent injection.

selected_finding.title: Permissive CORS policy with default-off authentication enables cross-origin data injection and exfiltration via the engram HTTP server

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory root cause and the copied PoC demonstrated advisory-level impact: a malicious origin can access the unauthenticated local server, persist attacker-controlled /learn content into the graph, and read graph metadata cross-origin.
evidence: cors-wildcard-csrf/vulnerability_report.json identifies src/server/http.ts json()/OPTIONS wildcard CORS, checkAuth default allow when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is unset, and /learn graph writes.
.judge/poc_runs/cors-wildcard-csrf/run.log shows Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on API responses and CORS preflight.
.judge/poc_runs/cors-wildcard-csrf/run.log shows cross-origin POST /learn succeeded with HTTP 201 and the injected proof string was found in graph query results.
.judge/poc_runs/cors-wildcard-csrf/run.log shows cross-origin GET /stats returned graph metadata with HTTP 200 and no authentication was required by default.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Copied PoC ran against engram v2.0.1 and showed attacker-origin wildcard CORS/preflight, unauthenticated POST /learn HTTP 201, injected proof retrievable through /query, cross-origin /stats graph metadata HTTP 200, and no default authentication requirement.
